sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The player moved right 5 times and is now near the blue horizontal bar. Looking at the image, the player (white + green) is now to the right of the bar. The right blue square is at row 24-27, col 44-47. I need to check the player's current position and then navigate to the right blue square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Player position:")
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check right blue square
for n in nodes:
    if n['id'] == 1:
        print(f"\nRight blue square: id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

But wait, I need to enter the square, which means getting inside the frame. The frame is at col 44-47, row 24-27. The inner blue square is at col 45-46, row 25-26.

Let me try RIGHT then UP, or UP then RIGHT.

# Target frame: row 24-27, col 44-47
